name: The Martinez del Puerto by The Valley and return by Roldan — circular from Los Alcazares
name_original: Los Martínez del Puerto por El Valle y vuelta por Roldán — circular desde Los Alcázares
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Los Alcázares traverse a diverse landscape, from the flat coastal plains bordering the Mar Menor lagoon to the rolling inland "campo" characterized by citrus groves. The region offers smooth, low-traffic roads suitable for various cycling styles, including endurance rides and speed training. While many routes feature minimal elevation gain along the coast, more challenging options extend inland towards hills and mountain ranges, providing significant climbs. Best road cycling routes around Los Alcázares The most popular road cycling route is The Martinez del Puerto by The Valley and return by Roldan — circular from Los Alcazares, a 42.9 miles (69.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route ventures inland through scenic citrus groves. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Blick auf das Mar Menor – Castillo de Los Alcázares loop from RBS, a moderate 34.2 miles (55.0 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the Mar Menor lagoon. Local road cyclists also love the Lo Pagán Beach – Rotonda de los Flamencos loop from Los Alcázares, a 21.4 miles (34.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas with well-paved surfaces, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Road cycling around Los Alcázares is defined by coastal paths along the Mar Menor, inland "campo" with citrus groves, and access to rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal spins to more demanding inland climbs. The routes in Los Alcázares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Nearly 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Alcázares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Los Alcázares?
A: There are over 18 road cycling routes around Los Alcázares, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Los Alcázares?
A: Los Alcázares offers varied terrain for road cycling. You'll find smooth, low-traffic roads ranging from flat coastal plains along the Mar Menor to rolling inland 'campo' characterized by citrus groves. For those seeking more challenge, routes extend inland towards hills and mountain ranges like the Sierra Espuña, providing significant climbs.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or casual riders?
A: Yes, Los Alcázares has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. Many coastal routes feature minimal elevation gain and well-paved surfaces. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lo Pagán Beach – Rotonda de los Flamencos loop from Los Alcázares&lt;/a&gt;, which is 34.5 km with only 43m of elevation.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For more demanding rides, head inland from Los Alcázares. Routes like the 'Collado & Pedrera Loop' venture through citrus groves before a steady climb up Collado Ginovinos (7 km at 4-7% gradient). For serious enthusiasts, the Sierra Espuña Natural Park offers significant climbs, including the famous Category 1 Collado Bermejo.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Los Alcázares are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010163066/the-martinez-del-puerto-by-the-valley-and-return-by-roldan-circular-from-los-alcazares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Martinez del Puerto by The Valley and return by Roldan — circular from Los Alcázares&lt;/a&gt;, covering 69 km through scenic citrus groves.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Los Alcázares?
A: Road cycling routes in Los Alcázares offer views of diverse natural features. You can cycle along the picturesque Mar Menor lagoon, Europe's largest saltwater lagoon. Inland routes pass through scenic citrus groves. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Flats and Sand Dunes of San Pedro del Pinatar&lt;/a&gt;, a regional park known for birdwatching, or even the historic Torre del Rame watchtower.

Q: Is Los Alcázares suitable for road cycling year-round?
A: Yes, Los Alcázares is an excellent destination for road cycling year-round. It enjoys a warm Mediterranean climate with over 300 days of sunshine annually, making it suitable for outdoor pursuits even in winter. The smooth, low-traffic roads are ideal for winter training and endurance rides.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Los Alcázares?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' road cycling routes are not explicitly categorized, the numerous easy, flat coastal routes along the Mar Menor are generally suitable for families. These well-paved paths offer scenic views and minimal elevation, providing a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling routes in Los Alcázares?
A: The road cycling routes in Los Alcázares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic coastal paths to challenging inland climbs, and the smooth, low-traffic roads that make for enjoyable rides.

Q: Are there coastal road cycling routes with sea views?
A: Absolutely. Los Alcázares boasts seven kilometers of beaches and a long promenade along the Mar Menor, offering beautiful coastal cycling paths with stunning sea views. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5295812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blick auf das Mar Menor – Castillo de Los Alcázares loop from RBS&lt;/a&gt; provide scenic vistas of the lagoon.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling from Los Alcázares?
A: Yes, Los Alcázares serves as a great base for long-distance road cycling. You can combine various routes to create extended rides, venturing inland through the 'campo' or exploring the wider Murcia region. The 'Sierra Climbs Challenge' is described as a 'big day out' involving significant elevation across the Cresta del Gallo and Garruchal countryside.

Q: Are there any natural parks accessible by road bike from Los Alcázares?
A: Yes, you can access natural parks by road bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar Regional Park&lt;/a&gt;, located at the northern edge of the Mar Menor, is a haven for birdwatching and features historic salt mills, making it an excellent destination for a cycling route. For more challenging rides, the Sierra Espuña Natural Park is also within reach.
tips: Nice picture that we found rolling on the Murcian highway F-23 in good condition. The imposing Cabezo Gordo stands out, located at an altitude of 312 meters in the Campo de Cartagena.
In the town of Torre-Pacheco, we find this beautiful circular mill, 12 meters high, being one of the few in the municipality that is in perfect condition.

We are in the Murcian municipality of Los Alcázares, located on the banks of the Mar Menor, with beautiful beaches, natural landscapes such as the Marina del Carmolí or great gastronomy, the most typical dish being the cauldron, cooked with fish and rice.
